reviewText: Excellent product. The sun is extremely strong where I live, and I've been using this for several days now; it lasts a very long time on the skin. However, if you have very sensitive skin, I wouldn't recommend using it on your face—in my case, it caused a breakout.... Excellent product. The sun is extremely strong where I live, and I've been using this for several days now; it lasts a very long time on the skin. However, if you have very sensitive skin, I wouldn't recommend using it on your face—in my case, it caused a breakout. But for the neck, body, and arms, it’s excellent. I highly recommend it, and it doesn't leave a bad feel on the skin or an unpleasant smell. If you use it in the water, it is recommended to reapply every hour and 20 minutes, otherwise you might get sunburned.

reviewText: I’ve been really impressed with this sunscreen—it’s become a daily staple for my face and hands. The SPF 70 gives great peace of mind, especially with regular sun exposure, and it feels lightweight rather than heavy or greasy. It absorbs quickly and doesn’t leave that... I’ve been really impressed with this sunscreen—it’s become a daily staple for my face and hands. The SPF 70 gives great peace of mind, especially with regular sun exposure, and it feels lightweight rather than heavy or greasy. It absorbs quickly and doesn’t leave that thick white cast, which is a huge plus for everyday wear. On my face, it layers well under makeup without pilling, and on my hands, it doesn’t feel sticky or transfer onto everything I touch. I also appreciate that it holds up well throughout the day without needing constant reapplication (unless I’m outside a lot). Overall, it’s a reliable, high-protection sunscreen that’s comfortable enough to use every day.

reviewText: I try to always buy the highest SPF that I can find. This one fits the bill. If you apply it thoroughly all over and then reapply every few hours, you shouldn't get burned. I've worn it to the water park numerous times now. I have very sensitive skin, and it's... I try to always buy the highest SPF that I can find. This one fits the bill. If you apply it thoroughly all over and then reapply every few hours, you shouldn't get burned. I've worn it to the water park numerous times now. I have very sensitive skin, and it's given me no issues. I even put it on my face, just being careful not to get too close to my eye area. Once it absorbs into your skin, it doesn't feel too sticky like some other sunscreens can. Just be aware that it will leave a white residue on your bathing suit if you get it on there, but I've always been able to wash it out and that's a small price to pay for proper sun protection!

reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on March 30, 2026
reviewText: I'm new to skincare and I didn't know we were supposed to wear sunscreen! 😱 Turns out it's arguably the most important step of skincare. First, I like the texture of this sunscreen. It's not greasy, it's velvety and smooth. It feels nice on... I'm new to skincare and I didn't know we were supposed to wear sunscreen! 😱 Turns out it's arguably the most important step of skincare. First, I like the texture of this sunscreen. It's not greasy, it's velvety and smooth. It feels nice on my skin. I used this for the first time yesterday, and I just want those of you to know who are considering buying this, it applies well against makeup! That was one of my main concerns. I did my skincare with this sunscreen as a final step, then put on my makeup. The sunscreen acted as a primer, honestly, so I skipped primer. When I reapplied the sunscreen hours later, my makeup didn't smear! Instead I had a nice, healthy glow to my skin. I actually think it enhanced my makeup; my makeup seemed to last longer than usual. If you find yourself to be TOO glowy after a while, just apply a bit of translucent powder and your makeup will stretch even longer. Bonus: It fits nicely in your purse! Good for people like me with a million things they insist on carrying around.

reviewText: Shelf life is not great, I bought this in July 2024 and as of the last few months (it’s currently May 2026), it started to become watery/oily with a slightly gritty consistency (see photo). I have left this in a bag that got warm (at the beach/pool). That said, it seems to still be working because I’m not getting burnt. When I put sunscreen on my forehead and sweat, it tends to run down into my eyes and burn, this product seem to do that a fair bit less than others so that’s positive.

reviewText: I love this sunscreen! I have acne prone combination skin and there was no problems! I was looking for a new sunscreen after I was prescribed tretinoin and sunscreen is a must! It hasn't broken me out and have noticed my skin tone is more even. I was having issues of the sunscreen pilling so my advice would be to pat into the skin first and then rub to fully blend it in. It has that sunscreen smell that lingers after application but it feels so hydrating that I don't always need to also use a moisturizer, applied right after toner and serums in the morning.

reviewTitle: Best daily sunscreen I've used – lightweight, no white cast, non-comedogenic
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on May 11, 2026
reviewText: EltaMD UV Clear has become an essential part of my daily skincare routine, and I can't imagine going without it now. I was initially drawn to it because of the dermatologist recommendation and the zinc oxide formula, and after using it consistently, I can confidently... EltaMD UV Clear has become an essential part of my daily skincare routine, and I can't imagine going without it now. I was initially drawn to it because of the dermatologist recommendation and the zinc oxide formula, and after using it consistently, I can confidently say it lives up to the praise. What sets this sunscreen apart from others I've tried is how lightweight and non-greasy it feels on the skin. It applies smoothly, absorbs quickly, and leaves no white cast, which has been a dealbreaker with other mineral sunscreens in the past. It works beautifully as a primer under makeup and doesn't interfere with any other skincare products I layer it with. The oil-free formula is ideal for those with combination or acne-prone skin. I noticed that it doesn't clog pores or cause any breakouts, which was a concern I had when switching to a mineral-based SPF. The niacinamide in the formula is also a nice bonus for calming and evening out skin tone over time. The SPF protection is solid and the formula is broad-spectrum, covering both UVA and UVB rays. I feel genuinely protected wearing this daily, whether I'm commuting or spending time outdoors. The price is on the higher end for a sunscreen, but the quality justifies it. A little goes a long way, and the bottle lasts a reasonable amount of time. Highly recommend to anyone looking for a reliable, skin-friendly daily SPF that performs well under all conditions.

reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on May 25, 2026
reviewText: I don’t get the hype from everyone including dermatologists on this one! And no, it’s not the fact that it’s ‘toxic’ (according to some reviews here) cause a scammy app like Yuka that charges you to feed you bs and fear mongering told you so or that it allegedly damages the coral reefs or it’s not ‘clean’ (other missinformation and marketing scams). I didn’t even managed to finish the application as per recommended amount (yes, I measured it and for this one you need 2 pumps for face and neck) and it started peeling before I even got to the second pump. And not a little, like I slapped cottage cheese on my face! I thought it might be my skin care aka hyaluronic acid serum and skin barrier cream which are still oil free so I washed my face and applied it on bare skin. Still, the issue persisted! It would have been a dealbreaker already to not work with my skin care and therefore make up, but this also means that I cannot use it on my body either so that I wouldn’t have to return it. I was a little bit skeptical because it’s a hybrid sunscreen, and I am not friends with zinc oxide or titanium dioxide but I was hopeful for an elegant formula everyone is talking about that would make it work. Not buying into the hype again, back to my Roundlab and BOJ full chemical sunscreens.

reviewTitle: Lightweight, non-greasy SPF that actually works with short facial hair
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on May 19, 2026
reviewText: I added this as part of a simple daily routine focused on sun protection and long-term skin health, especially with regular outdoor time (tennis, walking, etc.). Broad spectrum coverage was a must, and I wanted something I’d actually use consistently. After using it daily, a few things stand out: * Lightweight and non-greasy. It absorbs quickly and doesn’t leave that heavy sunscreen feel. * Blends in cleanly. No white cast, and it doesn’t get stuck in facial hair. I keep very short stubble and a lot of sunscreens tend to clump or show — this one doesn’t. * Easy to use every day. Sits well on skin and layers without any issues. From a practical standpoint, this is the type of product you’ll actually stick with, which matters more than anything for daily SPF. Only minor consideration is price relative to basic drugstore options, but the texture and usability are noticeably better. Overall, if you’re looking for a daily sunscreen that doesn’t feel like sunscreen and works well with short facial hair, this is a strong option.

reviewTitle: Worth it to sooth and protect my red skin
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on May 13, 2026
reviewText: I have tried many face sunscreens (true sunscreens, moisturizers with SPF, etc) and haven’t found any I’ve liked — until eltaMD. It absorbs quickly, doesn’t feel heavy or sit on top of my skin, and doesn’t leave an overly shiny sheen. It’s unscented (no added fragrances) but the natural ingredients have a light pleasant scent. My skin is fair and I’ve developed some rosacea on my cheeks. The niacinamide is already (one week in) making a difference in the redness. Highly recommend as an easy way to finally incorporate that daily SPF! And yes, it’s worth the price!

reviewTitle: No chemicals that will be harmful!
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on April 19, 2026
reviewText: Ordered this mineral sunscreen looking for something simple and effective, and it’s been a solid option so far. The zinc oxide formula is the main reason I went with it. It doesn’t rely on the same chemical ingredients found in a lot of other sunscreens, which is... Ordered this mineral sunscreen looking for something simple and effective, and it’s been a solid option so far. The zinc oxide formula is the main reason I went with it. It doesn’t rely on the same chemical ingredients found in a lot of other sunscreens, which is a big plus if you’re trying to avoid that. It feels like a cleaner option overall. Coverage is good. It spreads evenly and holds up well, even with some water exposure. I’ve used it outdoors for extended periods, and it does its job without needing constant reapplication. It does leave a slight white cast at first, which is pretty typical with mineral sunscreens, but it fades in as you rub it in. Not completely invisible, but manageable. After a few uses, what stood out most is that it doesn’t have a strong smell and doesn’t irritate the skin. It just works without a lot of extras. Compared to standard chemical sunscreens, this feels more straightforward and less harsh, especially for regular use. For the price, it’s a great value. You’re getting strong sun protection without the added ingredients some people try to avoid. If you’re looking for a mineral-based sunscreen that’s effective and a bit cleaner in formulation, this is a solid choice.

reviewTitle: Not as safe as you think, do NOT buy.
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on May 25, 2026
reviewText: *5 stars only so people will see this!* This has chemicals like Butyloctyl Salicylate (It is suspected of disrupting the endocrine system as it can interact with female hormone receptors. Butyloctyl salicylate is also reported to have effects on fetal weight gain, according to a study reported to the European Chemicals Agency (ECHA) conducted on rats. Effects on blood clotting have also been noted. Analyses conducted by the Cosmetic Ingredient Review, an American cosmetic ingredient evaluation agency, indicate that the concentration in cosmetics can reach 35.9%. As a precaution, we recommend avoiding products containing this compound, especially for pregnant women. From an environmental standpoint, butyloctyl salicylate can cause long-term toxicity to aquatic life and the marine environment, particularly to small invertebrates and algae, which are the basis of the marine food chain.), Titanium dioxide (The IARC has classified it as a possible human carcinogen (Group 2B). It becomes particularly problematic when inhaled) and Alumna (Aluminium is a concern for several reasons. It is associated with effects on reproduction and foetal development. It is also suspected of being carcinogenic, genotoxic and of increasing the risk of diseases such as asthma, epilepsy or neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer's disease, multiple sclerosis or Parkinson's disease. In addition, aluminium production raises environmental concerns: it generates toxic substances that can be massively released into water, contributing to contamination and threatening aquatic ecosystems.). wtf!?!?! No thanks!

reviewText: Unfortunately this product did not work as advertised. My husband got an intense allergic reaction to this product. After a day of wearing this sunscreen, we ended up with red, itching, irritated rashes. We have both used many types of sunscreens (sprays, lotions, multiple brands) but have never had any issues with skin irritation. We won’t be able to use the rest of our purchased sunscreen as it caused such an allergic reaction and hives. I do not recommend this product.

reviewText: Works well for our whole family, including those with freckles and sensitive skin, and we’ve had no issues. Even our dermatologist and pediatrician use this now, they didn’t even know a stick like this was available 😎 It does go on a bit visible at first, but blends in once rubbed. Mineral-based with zinc oxide, so there’s no waiting, just apply and head outside. Lake and reef safe, using zinc oxide instead of typical chemical sunscreen ingredients 🌊 Vegan formula, cruelty-free, and free from parabens and fragrances. Smaller than a deck of cards🃏, so it’s easy to keep in a purse, backpack, or the car. Kids can easily apply it themselves, they call it war paint or game face, some even make a quick animal face with whiskers.

reviewText: Extreme white cast. I have light toned, sensitive skin. It’s common for sunscreens to cause a rash and sting my eyes, so I stick with all mineral formulations. The one I primarily use now costs $40 for 2oz so I’m on a quest to find a more affordable option. On the upside, this blue lizard feels relatively lightweight once dried and didn’t sting my eyes after 4 hours of wear. Not good is the classic white cast and paste-like quality. You have to really work to spread it around, and all that pulling isn’t great for skin. The white cast is still very visible 4 hours later on fair skin and it clings to dry areas emphasizing any flaking. It’s a no for me.

reviewText: I purchased this sunscreen because it is reef-safe and Hawaii-compliant, but after proper testing, it’s a no for me. Even when applying far less than the recommended amount on my face, not even close to the two-finger guideline, it left a noticeable blue-gray cast that was extremely difficult to blend. I had to wipe a significant amount off to get it to dissipate enough to wear, which defeats the purpose since I was no longer applying the correct amount needed for proper protection. The texture is thick, and blending takes a long time. While it blends in better on my arms and is more manageable there, it was not workable on my face at all. Sunscreen needs to be usable at the proper required amount to provide full protection. In real-life use, this formula isn’t practical, at least not for darker skin tones. I would not repurchase this product or recommend it unless you want to look like you belong in Avatar.

reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on January 31, 2026
reviewText: The AMOUNT of sunscreens I’ve been researching and checking all the ingredients is best to just save you all that time and energy with this review. I finally found this sunscreen that is cleaner for our bodies and environment with a high SPF at a decent price point (still definitely not the cheapest thing). Now let’s talk about the white cast. Other mineral sunscreens do have a stronger cast that make it hard for everyday wear. This one WILL leave a cast, but easier to buff into the skin so that is going to be a main reason why I’ll be sticking with this product for awhile. I still like to keep a look out on trying others that have clean ingredients, especially if I find one with an overall less cost as well. Fun note: The cap did change colors! Left it in the car on the dash. Cap has not changed when sitting out of the beach. Suppose to show how strong the UV is.

I purchase this brand/SPF strength every summer and one can lasts me an entire summer.  And I share with friends!  It does a great job keeping sunburn away and I can stay in the hot FL sun while on vacation pretty much most of the day!  I only re-apply toward the end of the afternoon to make sure I am not going to end up having a sunburn.  I live in SC and have a condo pool which we visit at least 2-3 times per week as well. This lasts a long time.  A great value for hte money!

reviewTitle: The perfect summer tint with SPF protection
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on May 22, 2026
reviewText: I am really enjoying this CeraVe Hydrating Mineral Sunscreen! I am always on the hunt for products that give me a quick, healthy glow without having to put on a full face of makeup, especially during the summer, and this completely hits the mark for easy coverage. ​The... I am really enjoying this CeraVe Hydrating Mineral Sunscreen! I am always on the hunt for products that give me a quick, healthy glow without having to put on a full face of makeup, especially during the summer, and this completely hits the mark for easy coverage. ​The texture is beautifully lightweight and glides on so smoothly. I was honestly a bit nervous that it would leave that heavy, sticky, suffocating feeling that so many sunscreens do, but it is completely non-greasy. Plus, a huge win for me is that after wearing it for hours, it doesn't migrate or burn my eyes at all. ​The only reason I'm knocking off one star is the shade range. I picked up the Medium tint, and while I can definitely make it work for the summer when I have a bit more color, I really wish they offered an "in-between" shade option between Light and Medium. ​If you're looking for a comfortable, daily SPF that lets your skin breathe while evening out your complexion, this is a fantastic staple to have in your routine!

reviewTitle: Exceptionally nice and light for older skin (with a couple of caveats)
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on May 18, 2026
reviewText: At 75, foundation can be a real challenge, too heavy and it settles into fine lines, looking caked on, and it will make you look way older than you are! I've always leaned toward a natural finish anyway, so when I discovered this tinted moisturizer at such a great... At 75, foundation can be a real challenge, too heavy and it settles into fine lines, looking caked on, and it will make you look way older than you are!  I've always leaned toward a natural finish anyway, so when I discovered this tinted moisturizer at such a great price I was super happy! For the most part, it delivers. It has a noticeable luminous finish that reads as "shiny" on my skin, and it won't do much to conceal age spots (though honestly, I'm fully aware, that's not what a tinted moisturizer is designed for.) A dab of concealer handles my brown spots and for the shine, a light dusting of mineral powder does the trick. The SPF is of course, a big bonus.  If you're an active outdoors person who wants effortless, natural-looking light coverage without the weight of a foundation, this is it.

reviewText: I really like this for everyday use. It gives a nice, even coverage without feeling thick or looking cakey like some tinted sunscreens or foundations can. It’s definitely not as heavy as a full cover-up, but it still does enough to smooth out discoloration and... I really like this for everyday use. It gives a nice, even coverage without feeling thick or looking cakey like some tinted sunscreens or foundations can. Overall, a great option if you want light coverage with a more natural look instead of a full makeup feel. I use light in the winter and light medium in the summer.

reviewTitle: Doesn’t stink, not super thick and minimal white cast
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on April 3, 2026
reviewText: The product is a lot thinner than other sunblocks. VERY creamy! The tint is sheer but it’s less sheer than I actually thought. Which is fine bc I’ll use it as more natural makeup in the summer time if I go anywhere. It’s not so much that you can tell either though! My... The product is a lot thinner than other sunblocks. VERY creamy! The tint is sheer but it’s less sheer than I actually thought. Which is fine bc I’ll use it as more natural makeup in the summer time if I go anywhere. It’s not so much that you can tell either though! My sister who sees me every single day told me my skin looked “very youthful” today and didn’t know it was tinted spf. She had smelled my face when I wore another spf and had agreed the other one STUNK so badly but we didn’t smell anything on this one. I am fairly light but I got the medium based on reviews I searched for on tiktok. I figured the white cast would lighten it up somehow but there is NO WHITE CAST! But the color is still fine bc it isn’t chalky like la Roche posay tinted liquid spf. This is very natural! When I applied this product it looked SO SHINY. I almost regretted it…. But 20-30 min later (I applied it then immediately drove 15 min to my sisters house) the product had set and it has a much more natural and dewy but not crazy oily finish. I love it so far. I do consider myself to have very sensitive dry/combo skin and am in the process of repairing a damaged skin barrier. This didn’t itch or burn or anything. I did notice it started pulling after rubbing it in so much but I did kinda rush my morning routine this morning. I’ll update if it wasn’t my bad. Update: it’s been 5 hrs since I applied it. It’s definitely not going to be doubling as a moisturizer. I don’t feel like it’s dehydrated but it’s not moisturizing like other sun screen has been. But maybe just expected it to since it’s cerave but it’s not marketed as such.  But that’s ok with me bc I’m more worried about the SPF part of the product. Shade: at first I didn’t notice bc I was just indoors…. But it has enough tint for someone to notice the change in skin tone between my face and body so now I’m going to need to order the light one

reviewTitle: Lightweight, Natural Glow and Perfect for Sensitive Skin
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on April 1, 2026
reviewText: I’ve been using this tinted mineral sunscreen and I’m really impressed so far. The texture is lightweight, easy to apply, and blends nicely into the skin without feeling heavy or greasy. It gives a subtle, natural glow that helps even out the skin tone without looking like... I’ve been using this tinted mineral sunscreen and I’m really impressed so far. The texture is lightweight, easy to apply, and blends nicely into the skin without feeling heavy or greasy. It gives a subtle, natural glow that helps even out the skin tone without looking like makeup. I have sensitive skin and this formula feels very gentle with no irritation at all. It’s fragrance-free and comfortable to wear throughout the day. I also like that it contains mineral filters like zinc oxide and titanium dioxide, which sit on the skin and help reflect UV rays rather than absorb them. (CeraVe￼) The tint helps reduce the white cast you usually get with mineral sunscreens and makes it perfect for everyday use, whether worn alone or under makeup. The packaging is simple and practical, and the size is great for daily use. Overall, this is a great combination of skincare and sun protection, providing hydration, a natural finish, and reliable SPF coverage. Definitely a great option if you want something gentle, effective, and easy to wear every day.

reviewText: I've never used tinted moisturizer before but I've been dealing with iron and vitamin D deficiency, and the supplements have been making my face breakout a lot. This stuff is pretty good for the price, I didn't expect it to give a bunch of coverage since it's only marketed as a sheer tint. I shared a before and after photo (took them in the same exact position and lighting) to show the amount of coverage it provided for me. I feel its just enough to even out skin tone and blur blemishes a bit. I also showed how much I used, which was actually a little bit too much, and I just rubbed it in thoroughly. It also feels great on my skin and seems hydrating...and it has spf, so even more benefit.

reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on April 21, 2026
reviewText: I’ve tried this today for the first time, so I will update after a month. I selected the medium tone. It’s sheer enough and works perfectly. It blends in seamlessly and has a nice consistency, which seems to melt into the skin. I personally love the dewy finish! At 61, I prefer to wear much less foundation these days, however, my skin needs more coverage, thanks to the pesky sun spots. What a conundrum! As a Make-up Artist, I’ve always told my clients to take care of their skin first. It’s the foundation for everything and a reflection of your health and well being. As much as I love makeup and the way it can boost confidence and enhance natural beauty, the option to go makeup free can feel liberating. I still do use full cover foundation on occasions when I can’t go without, but for every day this tinted SPF lotion a great solution. It’s non comedogenic and the price is great. It does blend away some imperfections, however, if you have spots you want to cover, you may need to mix in a little bit of concealer here and there. I highly recommend trying it! Don’t forget to choose Joy & Love ❤️

reviewText: I’ve been using this CeraVe sunscreen and I’m honestly impressed. The texture is lightweight and feels very comfortable on the skin—no greasy or heavy feeling at all. reviewTitle: Glowy, hydrating tinted sunscreen (best for dry skin)
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on April 8, 2026
reviewText: This has become my daily go-to tinted sunscreen moisturizer. I’ve tried more expensive brands (including medical-grade lines and color-adapting formulas), and I honestly keep coming back to this one because it gives my skin a really natural, healthy glow. I have drier skin, and this works well for me because it feels moisturizing and leaves a slightly dewy finish. It’s not matte at all—so if you prefer a shine-free look or have oily skin, this might feel a little too glowy. For me, though, it gives just enough tint to even out my skin tone while still looking natural and lightweight. For the price, I think it’s absolutely worth it, especially if you like that hydrated, “your skin but better” look.

reviewTitle: Great product But big safety concerns
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on March 11, 2026
reviewText: This tinted face cream is fantastic it blends into your skin and just leaves a healthy appearance. I wish they made a higher spf in tinted sunscreen. HOWEVER!!!! I don’t believe this is a new product!!! There was no seal around the cap of the tube, the box was crumbled and kinda used looking BUT worse. I don’t think the tube was full. The full amount. I had to press down alot to get anything out and then it was mostly air!! I would squeeze and squeeze and just get air!!! I ve only used it twice and I feel I will have to get another. NOW. This has been happen ending to me a lot with creams and face products shipped by Amazon. I really am beginning to question their safety. These are facial creams and liquids and should not be happening I will buy this again. But from a CVS or like where the product is sealed and I feel a little more trust!!!!!!!

reviewText: Goes on smooth and rubs in okay but not sure how this is considered “medium” tint. Probably because it’s still a mineral sunscreen. I compared it to the elta MD sunscreen (on the right) which i like but that one pills so moisture before but doesn’t leave a cast at all while still looking fresh faced. reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on May 3, 2026
reviewText: THE GOOD- It’s packed with ceramides and hyaluronic acid. The product glides effortlessly and doesn’t leave a white or gray cast on my melanated skin. It’s very moisturizing! THE BAD - The packaging could use improvement. It’s a gooey, globby mess to use. When squeezing it onto my fingers, to get two fingers length worth of SPF, you get way too much product. Then when you close the tube, the remaining product spatters everywhere. Just a huge mess. THE UGLY- It has no staying power. The product is so moisturizing and doesn’t seem to ever dry down. Blotting helped, but then I wound up with product transfer all over the tissue. That doesn’t give me much confidence that the SPF will offer maximum protection.
